article.description: Dalron Johnson scored 21 points and UNLV heated up in the second half to beat Utah 76-70 in the Mountain West Conference tournament semifinals Friday.
article.headline: Rebels reach Mt. West final vs. Aztecs
article.linkText: UNLV upsets second-seeded Utes in Mt. West
article.story:
LAS VEGAS (AP) -- Dalron Johnson scored 21 points and UNLV heated
up in the second half to beat Utah 76-70 in the Mountain West
Conference tournament semifinals Friday.

	   The third-seeded Rebels (20-9) meet San Diego State in the
championship Saturday night. The Aztecs earlier beat top-seeded
Wyoming 70-69.

	   Nick Jacobsen led Utah (21-8) with 16 points, while Phil Cullen
added 14 and Travis Spivey 11.

	   The Rebels shot 66.7 percent from the field (12-of-18) in the
second half. They led 62-61 with 3:50 remaining when Johnson scored
and hit two free throws 47 seconds later to put the UNLV up 66-61.

	   Jevon Banks' layup extended the lead to 68-61, but Jeff Johnson
hit a 3 and was fouled, converting a four-point play to pull the
Utes within 68-65.

	   Vince Booker countered with a 3-pointer for the Rebels to extend
the lead to 71-65 with 1:00 remaining, and the Utes didn't get
closer than five the rest of the way.

	   Chris Richardson scored 13 points and Marcus Banks and Jevon
Banks had 12 apiece for the Rebels.

	   UNLV jumped to an early 20-3 lead with 12:58 left in the first
behind Johnson's seven points and five from Richardson. Utah missed
seven of its first eight shots.

	   Cullen hit two 3-pointers and added two free throws to help Utah
cut the UNLV lead to 29-25 at the half.
